title: 2) Aging related substances in dermis
text:
Collagen
Collagen comes from Greek word ‘kolla’ meaning 'glue'. Collagen is the main structural
protein in the extracellular space. It is an important "building block" for the skin. It makes up
about 70% of the protein within our skin and helps our skin keep its volume. With aging, its
formation gets reduced after around 25 and eventually stops, so the skin loses its volume.
Ligaments and tendons are other types of connective tissues, which contain a lot of collagen
ﬁbers. Research has shown that Acupuncture can help rebuild collagen ﬁbers in ligaments
and tendons.7
Elastin
Elastin comes from the word ‘elastos’, which means ﬂexible. It is literally a highly elastic
protein in the connective tissue. It allows many tissues in the body to resume their shapes
like a spring or a rubber band. Collagen and Elastin are both connective tissues that make up
our skin. Collagen provides rigidity as a building block, while elastin allows skin resilience
as a suspension.
Silva, M., et al. Anti-aging cosmetics:Facts and controversies. Clinics in Dermatology, 2013;31:750-758.
6
Almeida MS, et al. Acupuncture increases the diameter and reorganization of collagen ﬁbrils during rat tendon healing.
7
Acupunct Med, 2015;33:51–57.

Facial  Cosmetic Acupuncture
Hyaluronic acid (GAG’S:Glycosaminoglycans)
Hyaluronic acid is a clear, lubricative substance that helps the skin retain collagen,
increase  moisture,  and  provide  elasticity  and  ﬂexibility.   It  is  basically  a  chain  of
carbohydrates that love to bind to water, giving it a stiff, viscous quality similar to jelly. As
we lose the hyaluronic acid with aging, the skin loses the ability to preserve moisture,
resulting in the visible loss of volume and ﬁrmness.
~ !  ~
23
Collagen gives our skin volume and rigidity as a building block.
Elastin  gives  our  skin  resilience  and  ﬂexibility  as  a  spring.
Hyaluronic acid gives our skin softness as a cushion.
